description: Model the projectile motion of a water rocket by writing and executing a Python script. Use an initial velocity (v0) of 30.0 m/s, a launch angle (theta) of 60 degrees, gravity (g) of 9.8 m/s², and a time step (dt) of 0.1 seconds. The model should follow the kinematic equations X(t) = v0 * cos(theta) * t and Y(t) = v0 * sin(theta) * t - 0.5 * g * t^2. Save the script to /home/ga/Documents/trajectory_model.py and export the results to /home/ga/Documents/rocket_trajectory.csv. The CSV must include the header 'Time,X,Y', format all numerical values to exactly two decimal places, and contain only rows where Y >= 0, starting from t = 0.0.
